findings.title: Briefing accepts uncorrelated input acknowledgements
findings.detail: `EvInputAck` advances the dispatch to `Working` and cancels the input-ack timer without requiring the ack's `InputID` to match the currently delivered `BriefInput`. Any stale or unrelated ack, including an empty `InputID`, can therefore confirm the wrong submission and let the run proceed after the actual brief/resume input was never accepted, defeating the bounded-liveness/ack correlation contract.
findings.suggested_fix: In `DispatchBriefing`, require `ev.InputID != "" && ev.InputID == s.BriefInput` before transitioning to `Working`; ignore or fail closed on mismatches, and keep the timer armed for the in-flight input.

findings.title: Protocol input rejection is retried instead of failing closed
findings.detail: `EvInputRejected` calls `dispatchBriefRetry`, so a protocol-level refusal is treated like a timeout/retryable delivery miss. The run-state-machine spec says a `Rejected` ack must route to the fail-closed liveness edge, not redeliver the same input. Retrying a rejected submission delays recovery and can repeat an input the driver has already refused.
findings.suggested_fix: Handle `EvInputRejected` as a terminal dispatch failure, for example set `DispatchFailed` with reason `input_undeliverable` or a specific rejection reason and cancel `TimerInputAck`; reserve `dispatchBriefRetry` for timer/stale delivery cases only.
